context.project_description: Add token usage tracking for LLM queries by implementing a BenchmarkHook that intercepts chat method calls across multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) and extracts token usage information from provider responses.
key_requirements: Create new module fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py implementing BenchmarkHook protocol
Intercept chat() and chat_stream() method calls on LLM providers
Extract token usage data from provider-specific response formats (OpenAI: response.usage, Anthropic: completion_tokens/prompt_tokens, Cohere: meta.billed_units)
Integrate token tracking hook into FitzKragEngine initialization via existing instrumentation system
constraints: Must use existing InstrumentedProxy and hooking infrastructure in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Cannot modify public APIs or break existing functionality
Must work across all current LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) via shared protocols
Implementation must be minimal overhead with no performance impact on core operations
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py provides BenchmarkHook protocol and InstrumentedProxy class for method interception; fitz_ai/engines/fitz_krag/engine.py contains FitzKragEngine with _init_components() method where providers are created; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines ChatProvider protocol with chat() -> str signature; fitz_ai/llm/factory.py provides factory functions for creating LLM providers
stakeholders: AI engineering team responsible for LLM integrations
DevOps team managing system metrics and logging

in_scope: Implement a TokenTrackingHook that extends BenchmarkHook protocol
Modify engine initialization to instrument LLM providers appropriately via existing instrumentation system
Add token tracking around chat() and chat_stream() method calls with provider-specific extraction logic
Ensure tokens are properly aggregated and visible in logs/metrics
out_of_scope: Non-chat LLM operations (embeddings, vision) unless explicitly requested

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/engines/fitz_krag/engine.py — FitzKragEngine - Knowledge Routing Augmented Generation engine
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py — implements TokenTrackingHook that extends BenchmarkHook protocol to intercept chat method calls and extract token usage from provider responses using provider-specific extraction logic

assumptions.assumption: The codebase already has robust instrumentation hooks in place via fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py that can be leveraged for token tracking
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, we would need to build a custom hooking system from scratch instead of extending existing infrastructure

assumptions.assumption: All LLM providers implement consistent method signatures through shared protocols (ChatProvider)
assumptions.impact: If provider interfaces vary significantly, the token tracking implementation may need to handle multiple different patterns and could become more complex or fragile

assumptions.assumption: The factory pattern for creating LLM providers in fitz_ai/llm/factory.py correctly handles instantiation of all supported providers
assumptions.impact: If the factory doesn't work as expected, token tracking might not be applied to some or all providers

assumptions.assumption: OpenAI's SDK provides usage metadata directly via response.usage with prompt_tokens and completion_tokens fields
assumptions.impact: If OpenAI API changes its response format, the extraction logic would break requiring updates to token tracking hook

assumptions.assumption: Anthropic responses include token counts in completion_tokens and prompt_tokens fields
assumptions.impact: If Anthropic's field names change or are missing, we won't be able to extract tokens from their API responses correctly

assumptions.assumption: Cohere returns usage statistics in response.meta.billed_units
assumptions.impact: If Cohere's metadata structure changes, token tracking for this provider would fail and require code updates

assumptions.assumption: Ollama has no direct counting capability but can be tracked manually by estimating input/output tokens based on text length
assumptions.impact: If Ollama starts supporting token counts in responses, manual tracking becomes unnecessary and potentially inaccurate; if it never does, we'll only have basic approximations rather than precise metrics

assumptions.assumption: The engine's _init_components() method is the appropriate place to register instrumentation hooks for LLM providers
assumptions.impact: If the integration point changes or if there are other relevant initialization methods, token tracking won't be properly applied to all providers

approaches.name: Extend Base Provider Protocols
approaches.description: Modify base provider interfaces to return structured responses containing token usage metadata alongside text responses.
pros: Provides a standardized way to capture and propagate token usage across all LLM providers
cons: Requires extensive changes to all existing provider implementations and their interfaces
best_for: Systems requiring comprehensive token tracking and observability

approaches.name: Add Token Usage Aggregation Layer
approaches.description: Implement a separate aggregation layer that wraps providers to capture raw API responses with usage metadata when available.
pros: Minimal impact on existing provider interfaces and implementation logic
cons: Limited by what each LLM provider's underlying API returns; OpenAI and Cohere support it, Ollama does not natively expose token data in simple string responses
best_for: Quick implementation that leverages existing provider capabilities where possible

architecture.reasoning: The recommended approach is to extend base provider protocols because it offers the most robust, future-proof solution for tracking token usage. This approach directly addresses root causes identified in the analysis: current ChatProvider.chat() returns only str without token metadata, instrumentation hooks cannot inspect token data from raw strings, Answer class lacks fields for token usage, and providers like Ollama don't structure responses to include token information at all. Extending base protocols ensures full compatibility across all LLM provider implementations while enabling proper integration with the existing instrumentation system that already supports wrapping method calls.

key_tradeoffs.extend_protocol_vs_break_compatibility: Extending the ChatProvider protocol to return structured responses with token usage would break backward compatibility with existing implementations that expect only string returns. This requires a careful migration strategy or versioned interfaces.
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_limitations_vs_provider_: The instrumentation system cannot capture token usage unless providers return it in their responses; this creates a dependency where either the provider interface must change to include metadata, or instrumentation logic must be significantly enhanced to parse raw output for token counts.

technology_considerations: Python typing.NamedTuple for structured response format
OpenAI and Cohere SDKs support returning usage information in their API responses that should be captured
Instrumentation system needs modification to extract token metadata from wrapped method results
Dataclass-based Answer object requires explicit addition of token tracking fields such as token_count or TokenUsage
architecture.scope_statement: Modify core LLM provider interfaces in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py to return structured responses including token usage metadata, update all concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) accordingly, and enhance Answer class to store this information for aggregation throughout the engine pipeline.

adrs.title: ADR: Modify ChatProvider.chat to Return Token Usage Metadata
adrs.context: The current ChatProvider.chat interface only returns a string, making it impossible for instrumentation systems or the engine to access token usage statistics from LLM calls. This prevents observability and cost tracking.
adrs.decision: Extend the base ChatProvider protocol and all implementing classes to return structured responses that include token usage metadata instead of plain strings.
adrs.rationale: The current contract in base.py defines chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str which loses token data. To enable tracking, we must modify interfaces to return typed objects containing both response text and token counts, enabling downstream systems like instrumentation hooks and the engine to aggregate usage.
consequences: Breaking change to provider interface requiring updates across all implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Instrumentation system gains capability to track token consumption per call
Engine can aggregate total tokens used for queries and answers
API layer can expose token usage in responses
alternatives_considered: Keep current interface and add separate token tracking mechanism - rejected because it would require duplicate logic and lose connection between calls and their actual usage data
Modify instrumentation system alone without changing provider interfaces - rejected because hooks receive raw string results that don't contain metadata, so no meaningful token extraction possible

adrs.title: ADR: Add TokenUsage Data Structure for Consistent Tracking Across Components
adrs.context: There is no defined TokenUsage data structure in the codebase. All LLM providers currently return strings with embedded usage information lost during processing.
adrs.decision: Define a structured TokenUsage class that encapsulates total_tokens, prompt_tokens, and completion_tokens fields to enable consistent tracking across all components.
adrs.rationale: Creating a dedicated token usage data structure provides type safety, clarity in code, and enables aggregation by instrumentation hooks or engine logic. It's necessary for making sense of provider responses before they're discarded.
consequences: New class definition required across multiple files
All LLM providers must be updated to return structured usage objects
Engine components can now properly aggregate token counts from different calls
alternatives_considered: Use raw integers or dictionaries instead of named tuples - rejected because they lack type safety and clear semantics for what each value represents
Do not track at all - rejected because this defeats the purpose of having observability in production systems

adrs.title: ADR: Extend Answer Object to Include Token Usage Metadata
adrs.context: The Answer class currently only stores text and sources. There's no mechanism to represent token usage that contributed to generating an answer.
adrs.decision: Modify the Answer dataclass to include a field for tracking token usage information from LLM calls used during generation.
adrs.rationale: For complete observability, answers should carry metadata about resource consumption. This allows users and systems to understand not just what was returned but how much compute went into producing it. The engine layer can aggregate this data when constructing responses.
consequences: Answer serialization may need adjustments if used in APIs or logs
Engine logic must be updated to pass token usage through the pipeline
API endpoints will have access to total token counts per query response
alternatives_considered: Add a separate metadata field to Answer - rejected because it would complicate access patterns and not leverage existing typing conventions used elsewhere in the codebase
Skip adding this feature for now due to complexity - rejected because observability is critical for production systems

adrs.title: ADR: Update Instrumentation Hooks to Capture Token Usage from LLM Providers
adrs.context: Current instrumentation system wraps method calls but receives raw string return values, which lose any embedded token metadata. Therefore hooks cannot extract usage information.
adrs.decision: Modify the instrumentation system so that it can inspect structured responses returned by updated providers for token usage data and aggregate accordingly.
adrs.rationale: Instrumentation is meant to provide observability into performance characteristics including resource consumption like tokens. Without the ability to see token counts from provider returns, these hooks are limited in their utility for monitoring costs or identifying hotspots.
consequences: Changes required in InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method to handle typed responses
BenchmarkHook interface may need expansion if more detailed usage tracking is needed
New logic added to extract token data during on_call_end events
alternatives_considered: Keep existing instrumentation unchanged - rejected because it fails to meet core observability requirements for LLM-based systems where costs are driven by token consumption
Create new hook system specifically for metrics - rejected because reusing existing infrastructure with minor extensions is more maintainable

artifacts.purpose: Defines the base protocols for LLM providers that all concrete implementations must adhere to
Provides instrumentation system for benchmarking and tracing LLM provider calls with hooks
Represents the final answer structure returned by query engines with text and source attribution

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Core Data Structures and Hook Implementation
phases.objective: Implement the token tracking data structures and instrumentation hook to capture LLM token usage per query
key_risks: Token structure varies across LLM providers and may not be consistently extractable
Instrumentation hook may miss some provider calls due to method name mismatches

phases.name: Integration with LLM Providers
phases.objective: Modify provider implementations to emit token usage data through the instrumentation system for tracking
deliverables: Enhanced OpenAIChat provider to extract and expose token usage from responses
key_risks: OpenAI SDK response format may not be present in all LLM providers
Streaming responses complicate token aggregation per call

risks.description: Token tracking hook assumes OpenAI-style response format for token extraction, which may not work consistently across all LLM providers
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific token extraction logic and add fallback handling for non-OpenAI providers
risks.contingency: Use a try-catch block to gracefully handle missing or malformed usage fields in responses, log warnings when tracking fails silently
risks.verification: Assert that TokenTrackingHook handles both OpenAI and non-OpenAI response formats without raising exceptions

risks.description: Streaming chat methods don't properly capture token usage due to incomplete stream processing before total tokens are available
risks.mitigation: Implement streaming-aware tracking logic that accumulates token counts per chunk or tracks completion at the end of streams
risks.contingency: Log warning messages when streaming responses don't provide usage data and skip token recording for those calls
risks.verification: Assert that streaming methods either capture partial usage data or gracefully fall back to no-op behavior

risks.description: Integration with existing LLM providers requires careful handling of SDK-specific response structures and may break if provider APIs change
risks.mitigation: Use duck-typing checks instead of strict type assertions when accessing usage fields from LLM responses
risks.contingency: Implement version compatibility layer that handles different SDK versions or API changes gracefully
risks.verification: Assert that the token extraction logic does not crash on different OpenAI SDK response formats

risks.description: Global singleton instance of TokenTrackingHook could introduce state management issues in concurrent or multi-threaded environments
risks.mitigation: Ensure thread-safe access to shared query_usages dictionary and use proper locking mechanisms where necessary
risks.contingency: Add unit tests covering concurrent access scenarios to verify that token tracking remains consistent under load
risks.verification: Assert that concurrent calls do not cause race conditions in the query_usages dictionary

recommended_contingencies: Implement comprehensive logging for token tracking failures with detailed error context and stack traces
Develop a fallback mechanism to track basic usage stats even when provider-specific extraction fails
Create unit tests covering all LLM provider response formats including edge cases like missing or malformed usage data
